descriptions.en: Former official landing paddock. Now closed except for genuine emergency landings.

Pod’s LZ provides a spacious area of flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze used for cattle grazing and haymaking. Be cautious of the power lines (marked) in the paddock to the west and possible rotor off the spur to the north. Walk out to Morses Creek Road via the (unlocked) gates taking care of the electric fences.

Andrew’s LZ provides a spacious area of near-flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze used for cattle grazing and haymaking. Divided by a fence, either of the two paddocks offers a safe landing. Be cautious of the adjacent (fragile) tea bushes. Walk out to Mt Porepunkah Rd via the drive running next to the tea bushes.

Paddock to the West of launch with the dam can be used. Make sure you have enough height to the power line that goes from the lane to the tower. Walk your glider out of the gate & pack up in the lane.
Hang glider LZ in the corner paddock on Murray Valley Hwy
The three paddocks directly below the SE takeoff. Avoid these paddocks if they are in use for cropping or have livestock in them.
Large open paddock with no fences or power lines.
Just to east of dam. Beware of the diagonal power line that runs across paddock to east of dam.

Jones’ LZ provides a flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze. Free of obstructions on the windward side, it offers a safe landing when the nearby Donahue's may be turbulent. Walk out via the gate at the north end and laneway to the main road. Say hello to Brian and Louise if you see them.

A spacious area of former pasture now being prepared for landscaping and a site cut for building leaving plenty of room for a safe landing into the valley breeze in the open area alongside the road. Walk out to Morses Creek Road via the (unlocked) gates taking care of the electric fences. Greet the owners Nathan and Hailley if you see them.
test

The Mt Beauty airstrip is a good place to land but watch for glider and GA traffic. If arriving high, lose height and land on the west side of the runway only. Gliders will do the same on the east side. Be aware that gliders may be towing into the north using a stationary winch. In that case, stay clear of the airspace immediately above the airstrip until the glider has released and the cable has been wound in.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

airspaces.airname: EmgyLZ: Manny’s LZ (formerly Eagle Rise or Pines LZ)

descriptions.en: This paddock is currently used for calving - emergency landings only. Offers a large paddock with a range of safe options and a few shallow gullies to catch the unwary. Gates are located at the western end and in the middle opposite the hay shed. Avoid flying low over stock in the adjacent paddocks. The owner, Manny will frequently come to chat with the pilots. Greet him warmly, he is a lovely chap.
Use this paddock as the normal landing area is currently used for calving.
Tomasoni’s LZ provides a spacious, flat pasture with no hazards and aligned with the valley breeze. If there are cows in this paddock, choose any of the adjacent paddocks which are clear of stock.

Mt Emu landing paddock - Baird's. Not visible from launch. Usually full of horses, just land away from them. This is the only paddock where it is OK to land near horses. Granma Roper's is a better LZ for HG albeit further from Mt Emu than Baird's which is still the closest LZ and might suit PG's better.

airspaces.airname: NoLaunch: National Park - Parks Victoria managed land

descriptions.en: National Park - [Parks Victoria](http://parkweb.vic.gov.au/) managed land. No launching of an aircraft. Emergency landings only. Check [here](http://parkweb.vic.gov.au/explore/parks) for a map of Parks Victoria managed lands. Relevant act: [National Parks Regulations 2013 (SR 2013, no. 115) - REG 102](http://www.austlii.edu.au/au/legis/vic/num_reg/npr2013n115o2013349/s102.html)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

A safe landing option: the owner does not wish it to become a target but will tolerate an occasional landing. Consider it only as a refuge and use the nearby blue zones when safe to do so. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owner if you see him.
No Landing due to horse in paddocks.
Approximate path of powerline.

A safe landing option: the owner does not wish it to become a target but will tolerate an occasional landing. Consider it only as a refuge and use the nearby blue zones when safe to do so. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owner if you see him.

A safe landing option: the owner does not wish it to become a target but will tolerate an occasional landing. Consider it only as a refuge and use the nearby blue zones when safe to do so. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owner if you see him.

A safe landing option: the owner does not wish it to become a target but will tolerate an occasional landing. Consider it only as a refuge and use the nearby blue zones when safe to do so. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owner if you see him.
Do not land in this smaller paddock if there is cattle in it.
Slightly sloping down towards the main road. (This is the paddock you have to drive through to get to the hill.)
Landings on the beach in the creek estuary are prohibited. Just land in front of this area.
Use the stile at the park entrance to get to the road.
Don't land near the house.
DO NOT PARK AT THE LAUNCH
Be aware that pilots may overshoot the landing zone and opt to land in the launch area. Avoid leaving gear unattended
Set up your hang glider here.
Avoid landing here at all costs if possible

Martinez’ LZ provides two spacious, near flat pastures with no adjacent hazards. Use either of the marked paddocks avoiding any stock. Adjacent paddocks may also be used if necessary. Please exit via the gate behind the house. Thank Emilio and Connie if you see them.

Martinez’ LZ provides two spacious, near flat pastures with no adjacent hazards. Use either of the marked paddocks avoiding any stock. Adjacent paddocks may also be used if necessary. Please exit via the gate behind the house. Thank Emilio and Connie if you see them.

Offers a spacious flat paddock aligned with the valley breeze. Surrounding trees may create rotor in stronger winds. Cattle (if present) will be calm, simply land away from them. Use the gate in the northwest corner to exit onto the bike track. Please do not bring vehicles into the residential court. Make your pick up by the bus stop. Greet Chris and Nadina if you see them.
Reasonable flat. There is an unlocked gate on the border fence.
Do not park here.
Keep this area clear of equipment at all times. Paragliders may be stashed against the bushes if necessary

Lay out gliders here to launch, being mindful that other pilots may overshoot the landing area and require landing in the launch area. Do not linger in the area. Launch and clear the space for the next person as quickly and safely as possible.

airspaces.airname: Haz: Hang Glider Set Up Zone

descriptions.en: Use this area to hook into your wing, keeping in mind that other pilots may be passing through towards launch, and hanggliders may overshoot the landing zone.

Please keep this area clear of all equipment at all times. Paragliders can be stashed against the bushes, but keep in mind hanggliders need this area specifically for top landing due to the wind direction.

Circuit on the west (Little Mt Buffalo) side. Do not pass low over the airstrip. No VHF radio required unless towing. Regularly used by light aircraft, microlights, helicopter joy flights, radio-controlled model aircraft and occasionally sailplane aerotowing.
Paraglider LZ on Red Cutting Lane
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Hikers and tourists often cross in front of launch. Ensure area is clear before launching or landing
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Nobody likes a showoff. This goes for all the turbines.
Please avoid landing here, the land owner does not want his cattle being spooked.
Cleared area good for hang glider setup and landing.
This is a large square paddock. Private property. Horses and cattle sometimes present.
Beware of the high spanning power line that runs from a little ways down the spur from the lighthouse high point across the main Tooma road to the smaller foothills to the west of lighthouse.
To east of launch. Beware of power line that runs along the fence line that runs east on south side of paddock.

A spacious flat paddock aligned with the valley breeze with one large tree to avoid. Be aware of sprinklers in the adjoining paddock to the north. Exit by crawling under the fence to reach the Alpine Highway or (better) walk out past the house. Greet Manuela and Sylvana if you see them.
Only land here when it's not being used for sport.

Please be VERY vigilant if you are landing in this area. There are two very long power lines with no poles or markers. The power lines are each side of Grange Road as shown. You may be landing in this area if you are heading up the valley from Apex or you get low on the south side of Mt. Porepunkah.

Please be VERY vigilant if you are landing in this area. There are two very long power lines with no poles or markers. The power lines are each side of Grange Road as shown. You may be landing in this area if you are heading up the valley from Apex or you get low on the south side of Mt. Porepunkah.
Do not land here due to horses and do not walk through this area. If needed land near the ridge and walk out around the sides.

The area is frequented by horse riders and pilots should not land in the red zone. Landing in the areas adjacent should only be considered when there are no horses in the area, or in emergency situations

airspaces.airname: NoLZ: Former Eagle Rise primary landing

descriptions.en: Guard dogs on property.

Harper’s LZ – offers a spacious flat paddock aligned with the valley breeze. Their horse is very calm and will not be of concern. Exit via the gate onto Harper’s Lane. Greet Helen and Grahame if you see them.
Full of blackberries. Can be difficult to reach in strong wind. Avoid.
Windsock in the middle.
Approximate position.
Power line runs from high ground to several paddocks across - beware.
Dangerous powerline up to 50m above valley floor. A powerline runs east-west high across the Sarabah valley, from ridge to ridge. It is to the left of launch, about 1.5km NE.

Tomasoni’s LZ provides a spacious, flat pasture with no hazards and aligned with the valley breeze. If there are cows in this paddock, choose the paddocks to the south if also clear of stock. Avoid the adjacent paddock to the north which has sprinklers fitted.
Emergency Only
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

Ray’s LZ provides a somewhat rolling pasture best aligned for a westerly breeze. Best landing area is at the western end of the property. Be aware of the single wire power line entering the property from the south towards the tin shed. Leave by the driveway at the eastern end of the property.

Schifferle's LZ provides a spacious area of near-flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze used for cattle grazing and haymaking. Divided by the driveway, either of the two paddocks offers a safe landing though the southerly one has better egress in that a gate in the westerly corner opens onto the driveway. Be cautious of the electric fences. Walk out to GAR via the driveway. Greet the owners if you see them.

Schifferle's LZ provides a spacious area of near-flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze used for cattle grazing and haymaking. Divided by the driveway, either of the two paddocks offers a safe landing though the southerly one has better egress in that a gate in the westerly corner opens onto the driveway. Be cautious of the electric fences. Walk out to GAR via the driveway. Greet the owners if you see them.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

airspaces.airname: NoFly: No fly zone unless above launch altitude

descriptions.en: The new house has requested we do not land at this property.

airspaces.airname: LZ: Alternate Mt Emu LZ - Granma Roper's

descriptions.en: We have permission to land at "Granma Roper's" on Mountain Creek Rd. The owner is Peter Roper, who has asked only that the gate is left locked. He's happy that we keep a windsock fixed to the fence as in the past. Granma Roper's is a better LZ for HG albeit further from Mt Emu than Baird's which is still the closest LZ and might suit PG's better. There is a power line running north-south along the eastern boundary to Granma Roper's. Ensure you inspect it before landing there and when you do give yourself enough height when arriving to keep clear of it. If landing here don't fly low over the triangular paddock immediately east, as there can be horses here. The recommended approach is to keep to the south of the Mountain Creek Rd bend when approaching to land into the north or west avoiding the horse paddock and powerlines as well.

airspaces.airname: LZ: Tawonga Gap LZ - Iaria's Paddock

descriptions.en: The Tawonga Gap LZ, Iaria's Paddock, is not visible from launch. It quite often has stock in it and the trees around the southern boundary have gotten quite tall. It is highly recommend pilots have a good look at it before committing to land there. Hang gliders generally land at the Mt Beauty airstrip instead.

DEECA Helipad and Firefighting Depot. There are regular helicopter operations from this site particularly during the fire season. The helos can be large and un-manoeuvrable due to water bombing apparatus. Be vigilant and avoid airspace over and around whenever possible.
If landing at Grandma Roper's don't fly low over this triangular paddock immediately east of it, as there can be horses here. Watch out for the powerlines.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Please stay clear.
A safe near-flat paddock with no obstructions set behind the caravan park. Avoid the earth works to the east of the LZ.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Horses in the area, no landing zone. If you land below the Thowgla ridge, please walk out away from this area.

A flat open paddock with a steep rise on the south side. Take care of the power lines which run along the foot of that rise. Leave via the drive and gateway onto the Porpunkah intersection. If conditions dictate, use the large paddock south of the power lines - you will just have to walk further.
Long Power line from high ground to low ground - no visible poles.
Please avoid flying low/close to the house and property at the old pines launch site. A sensitive landowner lives here. They are very keen to maintain their privacy.
Last landing paddock before the Kiewa Crossing. May be in the lee in strong SW conditions. Clear and suitable.

Landing beside the large towered power lines. Easy to navigate. There are several rows of stand pipe sprinklers running parallel to power lines. They are marked with streamers. (N.B. if you land here and walk past the Pianegonda house - please stop and say Hello - do not ignore farmers when they are close by - be polite and say Hello)

The paddock beside the house - a little smaller but still very suitable. High fences around the perimeter. (N.B. if you land here and walk past the Pianegonda house - please stop and say Hello - do not ignore farmers when they are close by - be polite and say Hello)
No landing or low flying. Sadly the low flying has been spooking the deer!
Do not land here during horse racing events.
Angry Male Farmer

The front south-east paddock is the best option: about six acres of clean pasture, it slopes gently to the south east. There is a gate into the drive and laneway in the west corner. The front north-west paddock offers a similar option of about three acres. Warning: A set of power lines runs up the driveway between the two paddocks. The hazard should be obvious to a pilot as fences have now been erected on either side of the driveway.
A set of power lines runs up the driveway between the two paddocks. The hazard should be obvious to a pilot as fences have now been erected on either side of the driveway.
A set of power lines runs up the driveway between the two paddocks. The hazard should be obvious to a pilot as fences have now been erected on either side of the driveway.

This is a long and narrow paddock but once you've down and on final low it's fairly easy to land in. The reports are that this landowner is fine for us to land there when there is no horse in that landing paddock. He has also requested that if you do land there that you drop into his house to notify him and thank him for the use of the paddock. One more thing to be wary of is the power line that runs across the paddock on the northern end. It's not a giant paddock but very landable (especially for a PG).
Horses, 100m agl minimum.
A spacious level landing with a stile for exit onto the airfield road. Stock are friendly but land away from them. Do not land within the airfield itself. You may find yourself locked in.
Paraglider landings only in emergency. Not suitable for hang gliders.
Lady mentioned issues with Ballons and have included us in that boat.
Now in the ownership of the Coffin family, the Wandi Pub LZ is open as a preferred landing area. Take care of the trees on the south border which have grown significantly since the site was closed.
Landings in this area parallel to the beach heading south east (away from launch) will always be approximately "in to wind".
Emergencies Only - care of cattle
Not suitable for hang gliders.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
PG bombout
Cattle and horses.
Avoid when stocked with cattle. Be cautious of the power lines in this part of the valley and particularly those (marked red) in the adjacent paddocks.
Three power lines spanning through the valley between ridges.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

Two spacious open paddocks which provide safe options if you are unable to make it back to the Mt Emu LZ. The paddocks slope gently down to Loudens Lane which borders the west side of the paddocks providing a gentle side landing into the valley breeze. Be aware of the power line (shown in red) which parallels the road at the foot of the slope. Take care for stock and walk out down the fence line to the gate opposite the Mt York Road turning. Retrieve vehicles pick up from the junction of Loudens Lane and Mt York Road. If you wish to sample the (tasty!) product of this and other local farms visit the Tawonga South Butchery.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
No landing at the end of the Pyramid ridge on the east of the Bright-Tawonga Road.
Benobble Powerlines
No landing or low flying – sensitive cattle.
No landing or low flying – sensitive cattle.

This is in major part flat pasture and a good landing option. The owner does not wish it to become a target. Consider it only as a refuge and use the nearby blue areas when practical. No retrieve vehicles are to enter the property. Exit via the gate which should be left as you find it. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owner if you see him.

This a long, flat pasture 80m wide at the northern end tapering to a point at the southern end. The strip is divided into two by a fence about half way up. There is room to land on either the north or south side of the fence. Use the gates onto the road and do not climb the fence. Observe the usual courtesies and say hello to the owners if he they are there or you see them in the first house on the left as you head back towards Bright.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

Two spacious flat paddocks to the east of the river divided north south by a fence with plenty of room to land either side. Walk out via the gate and driveway leading to the Gt Alpine Rd. Greet the owners if they are in residence at the house on the hill to the east.

Deer Farm LZ provides three spacious flat paddocks open to the valley breeze. Warning that the paddocks may at any time be occupied by deer who may be aggressive particularly from January to April. Choose an empty paddock. Landings are courtesy of the owners Rob and Grace Cavedon and their cousin Michael Cavedon. Say hello to the farm staff and bring the kids to visit the fascinating petting zoo.

Deer Farm LZ provides three spacious flat paddocks open to the valley breeze. Warning that the paddocks may at any time be occupied by deer who may be aggressive particularly from January to April. Choose an empty paddock. Landings are courtesy of the owners Rob and Grace Cavedon and their cousin Michael Cavedon. Say hello to the farm staff and bring the kids to visit the fascinating petting zoo.

Owner - Josh Hallright - 0430 150 510. The owner is more than happy for anyone to land on any of his property. Realistically, the front paddock is the only landable terrain with a slight slope towards the dirt road on the Northern end. This is Larkin lane and it is accessible with a 2WD from Mullagong Road. The gate is likely to be locked - please climb over the strainer post in the corner of the paddock as opposed to climbing over the fence or gate.

airspaces.airname: Powerline: Kevin Wheatley VC Rest Area

descriptions.en: Powerline from lake edge, up the hill

Mitch's LZ provides a near-flat pasture aligned with the valley breeze. Adjacent to Roger's LZ it provides a further option if caught downwind of Roger's. Be cautious of possible rotor in those conditions.

A large area of open pasture offering a safe landings into the valley breeze. Walk out to the access lane via the gate in the north west corner or through the car yard and down the drive. Greet the owners Russell and Marie if you see them.

Harrietville Sand Quarry offers a safe, level sandy landing. The approach from any direction is open with no obstacles but avoid flying low over the houses to the south. Be careful to avoid the (few) shallow craters in the sand that have resulted from the diggings. HG to assess the length of the landing for their purposes. Walk out to the west into the Tavare Park where there is room to pack up on the lawns.
If cattle are on Bill Skahill's paddock or he's growing hay then this next paddock north is an alternative with which the occupant of the house (his son) is also comfortable.
Horses.
Offers a large landing with gentle slope towards the valley. Take care of cross-wind thermals on warm days. Leave by the gate and the path past the Big Shed. Greet the owner if you see him.
A clear side landing comprising two paddocks with a fence up the middle. Take care of cross-wind thermals on warm days.
When making your approach, please keep well to the north of the boundary, set up over the tailings area itself and keep clear of the houses to the south.
Use the stile to get to the laneway back to the highway. Take care to leave the road gate shut if that is how you find it.
Land in the adjacent paddock if cows are in this paddock. Do not climb the fence, use the gate at the Harrietville end of the paddock.

Howell’s LZ, Ovens: Two spacious and level paddocks offer a range of safe landing options. Choose the paddock without the stock. There are gates in the western corner or south behind the barns, leaving via the driveway. Thank Nick and Alicia if you see them.
Official Mystic landing paddock. Not visible from launch.
Concerned Horse Woman
No landing or low flying – sensitive horses and cattle.
Contact NPC or NHGC for contact telephone numbers.
Contact NPC or NHGC for contact telephone numbers.

A very large area offering a range of options for landing. Hazards include a number of soft gullies and fences. The recommended area is a mown strip immediately behind Pat’s house (the one with the bus in the yard) or a little further north on the strip between the road and the first gully.

Easily recognised from overhead by the motor track, the reserve offers a range of safe options. Be aware to land away from the track itself should there be activity. The Reserve extends to the river which has a charming swimming hole. There is a clubhouse/bathroom at the top of the track. The managing Committee members live nearby. Greet them warmly if you see them.

airspaces.airname: NoLZ: Megalong Valley West No Landing
NoLZ: Lithgow Rifle Range No Landing

descriptions.en: Lithgow Small arms Factory Rifle Range No Landing

airspaces.airname: NoLZ: Lithgow Correctional Centre No Landing

descriptions.en: Mount Blackheath Emergency Landing Only
No landing – Private
No landing – Private
No landing – Private

airspaces.airname: EmgyLZ: Emergency landings only – sensitive stock.

descriptions.en: Contact NPC or NHGC for contact telephone numbers.

A large area of mixed pasture offering a number of near-level areas for safe landing into the valley breeze. Be wary of the deeper gullies. Walk out to Bohun’s Lane which can also be accessed by retrieve vehicles. The landowner is also comfortable with landings to the north of Bohun’s Lane. Greet the owners if you see them.

The Allen Brothers kindly allow landings in their safe, level adjoining properties. Beware of the power line which divides them and of the stock which rotates through the paddocks. Please thank the Allens if you should see them.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
No landing or low flight.

A spacious paddock with a shallow slope up to the west. Be wary of the EHT power lines on the north side and the domestic power line crossing south to north on the west side. Leave via the gate onto the main road under the trees in the top (western) corner.
The only good landing is a paddock to the left of launch, directly below the ridge. The paddock has some trees in it and is surrounded by fence lines. Phone reception is spotty.
Stock.
Extremely aggressive wedgie, do not enter unless you want new vents in your glider.
No landing on this driveway
This is the manager property, they ask us not to land or walk close by.
Nioka Range
Avoid landing here.
Short East LZ
Powerline South Launch
Avoid landing here. Blasting danger. No public access.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

Landing is ONLY allowed in the kidney-shaped paddock directly below the SE launch that often has cattle in it. There is a fence line surrounding the paddock and one directly dissecting the paddock. Watch out for powerlines near the road. There is usually no phone reception in the landing area. Access is via the river crossings on Condamine River Road, which is often closed (requiring a footmarch through the river crossings).

Landing is ONLY allowed in the kidney-shaped paddock directly below the SE launch that often has cattle in it. There is a fence line surrounding the paddock and one directly dissecting the paddock. Watch out for powerlines near the road. There is usually no phone reception in the landing area. Access is via the river crossings on Condamine River Road, which is often closed (requiring a footmarch through the river crossings).

Landing is ONLY allowed in the kidney-shaped paddock directly below the SE launch that often has cattle in it. There is a fence line surrounding the paddock and one directly dissecting the paddock. Watch out for powerlines near the road. There is usually no phone reception in the landing area. Access is via the river crossings on Condamine River Road, which is often closed (requiring a footmarch through the river crossings).
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
614 Borah Rd, New Mexico 2346
Mark has prizes cattle to avoid in various LZ paddocks, please contact him before flight.

airspaces.airname: EmgyLZ: Bearded Dragon Emergency Landing - Horses

descriptions.en: Don't land on the race course itself. Landing in the centre is ok, but watch for the powerline through the middle.
Alternative to Mystic LZ. Stock are friendly but land away from them.
Preferred LZ if landing short of the principal LZ’s. Take care of stock or crops.
Angry Farmer
Beechmont Top Landing. Walk out via track beside Orchard. Walk from the bottom of gully direct to property entrance.
Designated beechmont bombout, walking track to launch from the NW corner of the shelf. The walk out from the Big Shelf landing area is a 35-40 minute walk up through the club land back to the launch.
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)
Runs along the bottom of the hill and up the ridge to the south
Imported from the 'Landing and No Landing Polygons' section of [http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps](http://www.chgc.asn.au/Resources/Online-Maps)

other

Avoid landing here due to horses. If needed, land near the ridge and walk out around the sides of the no landing area.
Runs up the hill to the owners house
